extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked on the final category 2 climb, soloed to victory while ROCHE Nicolas limited losses to take red jersey by 2 seconds
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA attacked final climb with 500m remaining, refusing celebration until maximizing time gaps, racing through line for bonifications
ROCHE Nicolas feigned cramping at group front before attacking, tactical deception to discourage chase while positioning for overall
Movistar and UAE teams aggressively prioritized stage hunting over jersey defense after previous day's crashes, reversing typical GC conservatism
FORMOLO Davide showed poor positioning judgment, energetic but missing decisive move he was perfectly suited for tactically

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: attacked final 500m alone, rode through finish line for maximum time, swagger indicates peak form returning
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: positive: faked cramping to deceive rivals before attacking, gambling paid off with red jersey despite sore body from prior crash
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: finished third but appeared under pressure at back of lead group before final climb
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: negative: energetic but strategically poor, missed decisive move he should have been in according to commentary
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: lost 37 seconds and red jersey despite sitting in protected fourth position before final climb
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: present in lead group but not animating, riding defensively in white UAE colors
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: neutral: contributed to Colombian chase but refused extended pull, prioritizing own position over teammate QUINTANA
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: responded to attacks but finished 10th, shepherding group home rather than contesting stage
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: neutral: led chase group across line 37 seconds down in pink EF colors
